name: Prefab Labour Colony
description:
Prefab Labour Colony — Complete Factory-Built Facility for 100 to 500+ Workers

A prefab labour colony is the procurement choice you make when your project needs a complete worker accommodation facility built off-site, quality-checked at the factory, and dispatched as a single coordinated package. SAMAN supplies prefab labour colony arrangements for 100 to 500+ workers on infrastructure and industrial projects across India — sleeping blocks, dining facility, sanitation blocks, and worker recreation space, all manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and delivered together.

How Your Labour Colony Is Built Off-Site, Before It Reaches Your Project Land

Every prefab labour colony at SAMAN is fabricated, assembled, and quality-checked at the factory. You receive pre-finished units, not raw construction.

The build begins with MS frame fabrication on jig stations using IS 2062 Grade A steel. Insulated PUF sandwich panels are cut to size, edge-sealed, and fitted to the frame. PPGI roofing is laid with overlap seals and drainage tested. Wiring conduits, lighting points, and ventilation openings go in before the unit leaves the line.

MS frame fabrication on jig stations at the SAMAN Bangalore factory — every unit built before it reaches your site.

Every unit passes 52 quality checkpoints before dispatch — frame square, panel adhesion, roof seal, electrical continuity, door-window alignment. Your colony arrives in batched truck loads sequenced for on-site assembly. You assemble. You do not build.

What Ships in a Complete Prefab Labour Colony?

A complete prefab labour colony from SAMAN includes four coordinated facility types: sleeping dormitories for worker rest, a dining facility with cooking provisions, sanitation blocks with toilets and bathing points, and worker recreation space for off-shift use. Every facility ships pre-finished from the factory and is sequenced for on-site assembly in 10 to 14 days.

Four coordinated facility types — sleeping, dining, sanitation, recreation — dispatched as one complete colony order.

That fourth element — recreation — is what separates a real worker colony from a site camp. Long-duration projects need space where workers gather off-shift: covered seating, a TV or radio point, an outdoor patch for evening use. Suppliers who skip recreation are quoting a camp, not a colony, whatever their page title says.

Dormitories ship in 8 to 12 worker capacities. Dining blocks seat 30 to 50% of total capacity per sitting. Sanitation blocks follow BOCW Act ratios for toilets and bathing points. The layout drawing is issued at the order-confirmation stage.

Sized for 100 to 500+ Worker Projects — When This Configuration Fits

The prefab labour colony arrangement is engineered for the 100 to 500+ worker tier. Below 100 workers, the coordination cost of a four-facility setup is harder to justify. Above 500, configurations move to phased multi-block deployments.

At 100 to 200 workers, you need 10 to 25 dormitory units, 4 to 8 washroom blocks, 1 to 2 dining units, and a recreation block. Single-storey layout works on 50m × 40m of flat land.

Double-storey stacking — standard for 300 to 500 worker tiers to reduce site footprint.

At 300 to 500 workers, double-storey dormitory stacking reduces footprint, with two dining sittings and 6 to 10 washroom blocks. Phased dispatch is normal — three to four batches over 14 to 21 working days.

For shorter-duration projects where a complete colony is more than you need, our prefab labor camps page covers the standard prefab camp setup.

Built for Projects Running 18 Months and Longer

The prefab labour colony makes the strongest economic sense for projects running 18 months and longer. Three reasons.

First, fitout grade. Colonies at this duration need washable internal finishes, screened ventilation, sealed roof joints rated for two-monsoon exposure, and door hardware specified for repeated daily use. SAMAN colony units ship with these as standard.

Built for 18+ month projects — washable finishes, two-monsoon-rated joints, 20 to 25 year structural lifespan.

Second, worker retention. Long-duration projects depend on the same workforce returning shift after shift. Comfortable sleeping, hygienic sanitation, and a real recreation space measurably reduce shift absenteeism.

Third, asset value. Factory-built units hold value across redeployments — relevant when the colony moves to your next 18+ month project. Structural lifespan with standard maintenance is 20 to 25 years.

For projects under 12 months, our labor camps page covers shorter-duration alternatives.

Quote and Coordinated Dispatch — What We Need From You

Five inputs let us quote a complete prefab labour colony within 24 to 48 hours:

  • Worker count and shift pattern (single or double shift)
  • Project duration in months
  • Land dimensions and ground condition
  • Project location and road access for truck delivery
  • Required site readiness date

You receive a layout drawing showing dormitory placement, dining position, sanitation distribution, and recreation siting — with facility counts, total covered area, and the assembly sequence by batch.

Coordinated dispatch — dormitories first, sanitation ready before move-in, dining by day eight, recreation last.

Coordinated dispatch means each truck arrives in the correct order — dormitories first, sanitation blocks ready before the workforce moves in, dining commissioned by day eight, recreation last. One coordinated colony order, not four separate vendors.

If your project sits below 100 workers or your accommodation need is shorter, the broader labor colony page covers configurations and capacity planning from 50 workers upward.

Standard delivery is 7 to 21 working days from confirmed order.

Manufacturing facilities at Bangalore and Greater Noida serve infrastructure projects across India.

For a complete prefab labour colony quote, send your worker count and project duration to +91 97089 89937 or the enquiry form.

FAQs About Prefab Labour Colony

Q1. What's the difference between a prefab labour colony and a labour camp?

A prefab labour colony is a complete four-facility arrangement — sleeping, dining, sanitation, and recreation — engineered for projects running 18 months or longer with 100 to 500+ workers. A labour camp is typically a project-deployment-stage accommodation setup, smaller in facility scope or shorter in expected service life. The recreation facility and long-duration fitout grade are the colony's defining inclusions.

Q2. What recreation provision is included as standard in a SAMAN prefab labour colony?

A standard recreation block is sized at 60 to 120 square feet per 100 workers and ships with internal seating, electrical points for lighting and ceiling fans, and provision for a TV or radio mount. The outdoor recreation patch — usually a paved or compacted area beside the block — is confirmed during the site layout drawing stage.

Q3. How long does on-site assembly take for a 300-worker prefab labour colony?

A 300-worker prefab labour colony is assembled in 14 to 18 working days across three batched truck deliveries. Dormitories and sanitation blocks sequence first so workers can move in by day 10 to 12; dining and recreation blocks commission on the following batch. The site needs levelled ground and clear truck access for batched dispatch.
